name: Construction System
summary: Steward, Settlers, Settlements, Territories, HealthOS Forge MCP doctrine, repository-local construction roots, and agent infrastructure.
invariants: Steward, Settlers, Settlements, Territories, and HealthOS Forge MCP are outside the HealthOS clinical/runtime hierarchy.
Official docs remain canonical; derived memory and construction records remain subordinate.
Construction tooling has no merge, clinical, Core-law, regulatory, or production-readiness authority.
HealthOS Forge MCP and healthos-forge-mcp naming supersede deprecated repository-maintenance MCP naming.
allowedWork: Maintain construction-system docs, schemas, territory records, future Settler profiles, and future Settlement records.
Maintain deterministic healthos-steward behavior only where implemented and validated.
Prepare future HealthOS Forge MCP doctrine without claiming implementation.
Update trackers and handoff notes for construction tasks.
forbiddenWork: Make construction tooling clinical runtime or Core law.
Give Steward or Settlers merge, clinical, regulatory, or autonomous execution authority.
Use deprecated repository-maintenance MCP naming as canonical.
Claim HealthOS Forge MCP, prompt generation, PR review engine, or Settler execution exists before implementation.
validationCommands: for f in .healthos-settler/territories/*.json; do python3 -m json.tool "$f" >/dev/null; done
knownGaps: Settler Profile Registry is not implemented.
Steward CLI does not consume territories yet.
Prompt generation, PR review, derived memory builder, and HealthOS Forge MCP remain future work.
notes: This ST-011 registry is construction metadata only.
The existing `ts/agent-infra/mcp-local` path is not renamed in ST-011.
